required_changes: Disable auto_create_subnetworks before exporting subnets
Convert GCP region-based routing to AWS route table per subnet
Enable DNS hostnames and DNS support on AWS VPC
manual_actions: Verify CIDR ranges do not overlap with existing AWS VPCs
Recreate VPC peering connections using AWS VPC Peering
rules.rationale: GCP VPC networks map directly to AWS VPCs. Both are regional software-defined networks with CIDR-based routing. GCP auto_create_subnetworks must be disabled before migration; AWS VPCs require explicit subnet creation.

required_changes: Assign each subnet to a specific AWS Availability Zone
Create route table and associate with subnet
Enable map_public_ip_on_launch for public-facing subnets
manual_actions: Decide AZ placement for each migrated subnet
Create internet gateway if subnet needs public access
rules.rationale: GCP subnets are regional resources with a single primary CIDR. AWS subnets are AZ-scoped. Each GCP subnet must be split into at least two AWS subnets across AZs for high availability.

required_changes: Convert GCP network tags to AWS security group associations
Translate GCP priority-based rules to AWS stateful rules
Replace source_tags with source security group references
manual_actions: Map GCP network tags to AWS security group membership per instance
Review egress rules: GCP allows all egress by default, AWS requires explicit allow
rules.rationale: GCP firewall rules are VPC-level and target instances via network tags. AWS security groups are instance-attached and stateful. Each GCP firewall rule becomes one AWS security group rule; tag-based targeting becomes security group membership.

required_changes: Allocate Elastic IP for AWS NAT Gateway
Place NAT Gateway in public subnet
Update private subnet route tables to route 0.0.0.0/0 to NAT Gateway
manual_actions: Verify NAT Gateway quota in target AWS region
Configure VPC endpoint for S3/DynamoDB to avoid NAT costs
rules.rationale: GCP Cloud NAT provides outbound internet access for private instances. AWS NAT Gateway serves the same purpose. Both are regional, managed, and support port address translation.

required_changes: Create Customer Gateway representing the remote peer
Recreate BGP sessions with AWS ASN
Update on-premises VPN device configuration
manual_actions: Coordinate downtime window with network team for VPN cutover
Test connectivity before decommissioning GCP VPN
rules.rationale: GCP Cloud VPN tunnels map to AWS Site-to-Site VPN. Both support IKEv2 and BGP routing. Shared secrets and BGP ASNs must be reconfigured on both sides.

required_changes: Accept peering connection from the peer VPC account
Update route tables on both sides to include peer CIDR
Configure security groups to allow traffic from peer CIDR
manual_actions: Coordinate with peer VPC owner to accept peering request
Verify no overlapping CIDR blocks exist between peered VPCs
rules.rationale: GCP VPC peering maps to AWS VPC Peering Connections. Both enable private routing between VPCs without traversing the internet.

required_changes: Create separate route tables for public and private subnets
Associate route tables with appropriate subnets
Add internet gateway route for public subnets
manual_actions: Review all custom routes for GCP-specific next-hop types
Recreate routes pointing to GCP instances as AWS ENI routes
rules.rationale: GCP routes map to AWS route table entries. GCP uses a single routing table per VPC; AWS has per-subnet route tables.

required_changes: Map GCP machine type to closest AWS instance type
Convert GCP instance metadata to AWS EC2 user data or SSM parameters
Replace GCP service account with AWS IAM instance profile
manual_actions: Create machine image (AMI) from existing GCP disk or reinstall OS
Validate application startup scripts work in AWS environment
rules.rationale: GCP Compute Engine instances map directly to AWS EC2 instances. Machine types must be mapped to equivalent instance families. GCP metadata startup scripts map to AWS user_data.

required_changes: Replace GKE node pools with EKS managed node groups
Switch from Workload Identity to IRSA (IAM Roles for Service Accounts)
Replace GKE Ingress with AWS Load Balancer Controller
manual_actions: Migrate Kubernetes manifests: update storage classes, ingress annotations
Re-establish CI/CD pipelines targeting EKS cluster
Migrate container images to Amazon ECR
rules.rationale: GCP GKE clusters map to AWS EKS. Both run managed Kubernetes. Node pool configuration, networking (VPC-native vs VPC CNI), and IAM differ significantly.

required_changes: Replace GCP client libraries with AWS SDK calls
Map Cloud Pub/Sub triggers to SQS/SNS event sources
Replace Cloud Storage triggers with S3 event notifications
manual_actions: Audit all GCP-specific API calls in function code
Test cold start behavior differences between GCP and AWS Lambda
rules.rationale: GCP Cloud Functions and Cloud Run map to AWS Lambda. Both support event-driven execution. Trigger sources, runtime environments, and SDK calls differ.

required_changes: Create ECS task definition from Cloud Run service configuration
Configure ALB or API Gateway as the HTTP frontend
Replace Cloud Run service account with ECS task role
manual_actions: Rebuild container image if it uses GCP-specific base images
Configure VPC networking for ECS tasks
rules.rationale: GCP Cloud Run maps to AWS ECS Fargate. Both run containers without managing servers. Scaling, networking, and IAM differ.

required_changes: Map GCP storage classes to S3 storage classes (STANDARD->STANDARD, NEARLINE->STANDARD-IA, COLDLINE->GLACIER)
Convert GCP IAM bucket policies to S3 bucket policies
Enable S3 versioning if GCP Object Versioning was enabled
manual_actions: Transfer data using Storage Transfer Service or aws s3 sync
Update all application code referencing gs:// URIs to s3:// URIs
rules.rationale: GCP Cloud Storage buckets map directly to AWS S3 buckets. Both provide object storage with similar features. Storage classes, lifecycle rules, and IAM policies need translation.

required_changes: Map GCP disk types: pd-standard->gp3, pd-ssd->io2, pd-balanced->gp3
Create EBS volume in same AZ as target EC2 instance
Attach volume using aws_volume_attachment
manual_actions: Create snapshot of GCP disk, export to S3, import as EBS snapshot
Verify IOPS and throughput requirements are met by target EBS type
rules.rationale: GCP Persistent Disks map to AWS EBS volumes. Both are block storage attached to compute instances. Disk types map to EBS volume types.

required_changes: Create EFS mount targets in each subnet requiring access
Update /etc/fstab or mount configurations to use EFS DNS name
Configure EFS security groups to allow NFS port 2049
manual_actions: Migrate file data using DataSync or rsync over Direct Connect
Update application configuration with new NFS mount points
rules.rationale: GCP Filestore maps to AWS EFS. Both provide managed NFS file storage accessible from multiple instances.

required_changes: Map Cloud SQL tier to RDS instance class
Configure RDS parameter group to match Cloud SQL flags
Enable Multi-AZ for production workloads
manual_actions: Export data using Cloud SQL export, import using RDS restore or DMS
Update all connection strings to new RDS endpoint
Test application with RDS before cutover
rules.rationale: GCP Cloud SQL instances map to AWS RDS. Both are managed relational databases. Engine versions, parameter groups, and maintenance windows differ.

required_changes: Redesign partition key and sort key to match DynamoDB requirements
Replace Firestore collection/document model with DynamoDB table/item model
Convert GCP queries to DynamoDB expressions
manual_actions: Rewrite all database access code — APIs are not compatible
Migrate data using custom ETL pipeline
Load test DynamoDB capacity planning before cutover
rules.rationale: GCP Firestore and Datastore map to AWS DynamoDB. Both are serverless NoSQL databases. Data models, query languages, and consistency models differ significantly.

required_changes: Choose ElastiCache engine matching GCP Memorystore version
Configure subnet group with private subnets
Set security group to allow Redis port 6379 from application tier
manual_actions: Flush and repopulate cache after cutover — do not attempt live migration
Update application Redis connection strings to ElastiCache endpoint
rules.rationale: GCP Memorystore (Redis/Memcached) maps to AWS ElastiCache. Both are managed in-memory caching services with Redis compatibility.

required_changes: Create assume role policy allowing EC2/ECS/Lambda to assume the role
Map GCP predefined roles to AWS managed policies
Replace Workload Identity with IRSA or instance profile
manual_actions: Audit all GCP IAM bindings and recreate as AWS IAM policies
Security review required: permission models are not isomorphic
rules.rationale: GCP Service Accounts map to AWS IAM Roles. Both provide identity for workloads. GCP Workload Identity Federation becomes IRSA for EKS or EC2 instance profiles.

required_changes: Translate GCP permission strings to AWS IAM action strings
Convert resource-level conditions from GCP format to AWS IAM Condition format
Attach policy to IAM roles via aws_iam_role_policy_attachment
manual_actions: Manual permission mapping required — no automated translation exists
Validate effective permissions match original GCP role using IAM Access Analyzer
rules.rationale: GCP IAM custom roles map to AWS IAM customer managed policies. Action namespaces differ completely between GCP and AWS.

required_changes: Create secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with matching names
Update IAM policies to allow application roles to access secrets
Update application code to use AWS SDK SecretsManager client
manual_actions: Re-enter all secret values — values cannot be exported from GCP Secret Manager
Update all secretsmanager:GetSecretValue calls and SDK references
rules.rationale: GCP Secret Manager maps to AWS Secrets Manager. Both store encrypted secrets with version control and IAM-based access.

required_changes: Request certificate in ACM for same domains
Complete DNS or email validation in ACM
Attach ACM certificate ARN to ALB/CloudFront instead of GCP load balancer
manual_actions: GCP certificates cannot be exported — new certificates must be issued
Update DNS records for domain validation
rules.rationale: GCP Certificate Manager maps to AWS ACM. Both provide TLS certificate management. ACM certificates are free for AWS resources.

required_changes: Create ALB target groups for each backend service
Translate GCP URL map to ALB listener rules
Configure health check paths and intervals
manual_actions: Validate all routing rules after cutover
Update DNS records to point to ALB DNS name
Review SSL policy compatibility
rules.rationale: GCP HTTP(S) Load Balancer maps to AWS ALB. Both support path-based and host-based routing. URL map rules translate to ALB listener rules.

required_changes: Configure CloudFront origin pointing to ALB or S3 bucket
Set cache behaviors matching GCP CDN cache rules
Configure custom error responses
manual_actions: Update DNS records to CloudFront distribution domain
Invalidate CloudFront cache after deployment
Test cache hit rates match expected behavior
rules.rationale: GCP Cloud CDN maps to AWS CloudFront. Both are global CDN services. Cache behaviors, origins, and invalidation APIs differ.

required_changes: Create Route 53 hosted zone with same domain name
Export all DNS records from GCP Cloud DNS
Import records into Route 53
manual_actions: Update domain registrar nameservers to Route 53 NS records
Allow DNS TTL to expire before decommissioning GCP DNS zone
Test DNS resolution from multiple locations after cutover
rules.rationale: GCP Cloud DNS managed zones map to AWS Route 53 hosted zones. Both support public and private DNS zones with similar record types.

required_changes: Create records in Route 53 hosted zone
Update A records to point to AWS resource IPs
Lower TTL 24-48 hours before migration to speed up propagation
manual_actions: Validate all records resolve correctly before decommissioning GCP DNS
Monitor DNS propagation using external DNS checkers
rules.rationale: GCP DNS record sets map to AWS Route 53 records. Both support A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, TXT and other standard record types.

required_changes: Create SNS topic with matching name
Recreate subscriptions as SNS subscriptions or SQS queue subscriptions
Replace Pub/Sub client library with AWS SNS SDK
manual_actions: Update all publishers to use SNS publish API
Migrate subscribers to SNS subscription or SQS consumer model
Test message delivery guarantees match application requirements
rules.rationale: GCP Pub/Sub topics map to AWS SNS topics for fan-out messaging. SNS push delivery maps to Pub/Sub push subscriptions. Pull subscriptions map to SQS.

required_changes: Configure SQS visibility timeout matching GCP task retry window
Set message retention period and max receive count
Create Dead Letter Queue for failed messages
manual_actions: Drain existing GCP queue before cutover
Update all producers and consumers to use AWS SQS SDK
Validate message ordering requirements — use SQS FIFO if ordering matters
rules.rationale: GCP Cloud Tasks and Pub/Sub pull subscriptions map to AWS SQS. Both provide message queuing with at-least-once delivery.

required_changes: Map GCP metric types to equivalent CloudWatch metric names
Configure CloudWatch alarm actions (SNS notifications, Auto Scaling)
Set comparison operator and threshold values
manual_actions: Recreate all dashboards in CloudWatch — no automated migration
Update on-call runbooks with new CloudWatch console URLs
rules.rationale: GCP Cloud Monitoring alert policies map to AWS CloudWatch Alarms. Both trigger on metric thresholds. Metric names and namespaces differ completely.

required_changes: Create log group with appropriate retention period
Update application logging configuration to use CloudWatch agent or SDK
Configure log metric filters to match GCP log-based metrics
manual_actions: Historical logs cannot be migrated automatically — archive to S3 if needed
Update log-based alerts to CloudWatch metric filters
rules.rationale: GCP Cloud Logging log buckets map to AWS CloudWatch Log Groups. Both aggregate application and infrastructure logs.
